blocks.content: Session Six. Conflict and Repair.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: We started with how you fight. I want to go deeper now. You've had six months of being engaged. Has there been a conflict since we started that you'd like to bring in.
They exchanged a look through their respective screens.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Spring break.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Yeah.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: Tell me.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I was going to visit him in LA. And then a friend from my program needed help with something urgent — an essay submission, a deadline I can't fully explain without details — and I asked Kit if I could come one day later. And he said fine. But the way he said fine.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I was disappointed. I'd rearranged my schedule.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I know. But you didn't say that. You said fine and then you were quiet for two days.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: Kit. Why didn't you say you were disappointed.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Because I didn't want to make her feel guilty for something she couldn't control.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: What happened instead.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: She felt guilty anyway. And distant. And then she called me on it.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: Daisy, how did you call him on it.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I said: I'd rather you tell me you're disappointed than disappear on me. Because the disappearing hurts more than the honesty would.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: And.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: She was right. I said that. I apologized.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: How long from the conflict to the repair.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Two days.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: I want you to notice something. You repaired. That is not small. Many couples let those silences calcify. You moved toward each other. What I want to challenge — Kit — is the interval. Two days is long. Is there a version where you name the disappointment in the moment, even imperfectly, and you get that time back.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Probably.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: You are allowed to be disappointed. You are allowed to say: this is hard for me right now. That is not a burden to Daisy. It is the opposite. It is the thing she is asking you for.
